description: Early blight is a common fungal disease of tomato that typically begins on older leaves and can progressively cause defoliation and fruit damage.
symptoms: Brown spots usually begin on older lower leaves
Lesions often develop concentric target-like rings
Infected leaves may eventually die and fall
Fruit lesions may develop near the stem attachment

favorable_conditions.rainfall: Rain can promote disease development and spore dispersal
favorable_conditions.additional_conditions: Stressed and older plants are often more susceptible

immediate_actions: Remove heavily infected lower leaves where practical
biological_control: Consider registered biological fungicides where locally available

chemical_control.recommended: Preventive fungicide applications may be considered when disease pressure is high.
chemical_control.warning: Use only products registered for tomato early blight in your region and follow resistance-management and label requirements.

severity_guidance.low: Few lesions on older leaves. Monitor and improve cultural management.
severity_guidance.moderate: Multiple leaves affected. Remove severely affected tissue where practical and consider registered controls.
severity_guidance.high: Extensive defoliation or rapid spread. Implement integrated disease management with local expert guidance.

farmer_advice: Regularly inspect lower and older leaves. Maintaining healthy plants, crop rotation, and reducing leaf wetness are important parts of early blight management.
disclaimer: AI predictions should be confirmed when symptoms are unusual because early blight may resemble other tomato leaf diseases.
